name: Tandoori Turkey Kabobs
recipeIngredient: 1 small jalapeno pepper, seeded and minced (optional)
1 tablespoon minced or grated fresh ginger
1 turkey breast tenderloin (1, 1 1/4 pound), cut into 3/4 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine cumin, salt, cayenne pepper, cinnamon, and cloves in a small bowl; reserve half of the mixture for Raita.
2. Prepare Raita: Add garlic, jalapeno pepper, and ginger to the remaining cumin mixture in a bowl.
3. Place turkey cubes in a large bowl. Drizzle with oil and toss to coat.
4. Sprinkle the reserved cumin mixture over the turkey; toss to coat evenly.
5. Thread the turkey onto metal or wooden skewers. (If using wooden skewers, soak them in hot water for 30 minutes to prevent burning.)
6. Lightly oil a grill rack. Place the skewered turkey on the rack and grill over medium-hot heat for 7-12 minutes, turning once, until the turkey is no longer pink.
7. Serve the grilled kabobs in pita breads, if desired, with Raita.
Raita:
1 cup plain low-fat yogurt
3/4 cup finely diced, seeded, and peeled cucumber
1 1/2 tablespoons minced fresh mint
2 to 3 teaspoons honey
Combine all ingredients and the reserved half teaspoon cumin mixture from Tandoori Turkey Kabobs in a small bowl. Cover and refrigerate until ready to serve. Makes 1 1/2 cups.
